title: Bridge Essay: Repetitive speech elicits widespread deactivation in the human cortex: the "Mantra" effect?
concept: Inner speech, mantra meditation, focused attention, language as mantra.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Language/Mantra** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Language as mantra — inner speech, mantra meditation, semantic cognition, focused attention.
**Phase context:** Inner speech, mantra meditation, focused attention, language as mantra.

**Repetitive speech elicits widespread deactivation in the human cortex: the "Mantra" effect?** — Brain Behav (2015). DOI: 10.1002/brb3.346. PMID: 26221571. BACKGROUND: Mantra (prolonged repetitive verbal utterance) is one of the most universal mental practices in human culture. However, the underlying neuronal mechanisms that may explain its powerful emotional and cognitive impact are unknown. In order to try to isolate the effect of silent repetitive speech, which is used in most commonly practiced Mantra meditative practices, on brain activity, we studied the neuronal correlates of simple repetitive speech in nonmeditators - that is, silent repet
